external_sources.label: OpenAI Cookbook - Build an Agent Improvement Loop with Traces, Evals, and Codex
external_sources.finding: A durable agent improvement flywheel starts with traces, adds human/model feedback, converts lessons into reusable evals, and hands evidence-backed changes to Codex.
external_sources.loop_implication: Every weekly insight needs a captured evidence record, reusable acceptance test, and Codex-ready handoff before it becomes product work.

external_sources.label: OpenAI Cookbook - Build iterative repair loops with Codex
external_sources.finding: Iterative repair is strongest when each pass responds to the validation delta that remained instead of guessing everything up front.
external_sources.loop_implication: The fix stage must record what failed, the exact validation delta, and the re-audit result before implementation is accepted.

external_sources.label: OpenAI API Docs - Evaluate agent workflows
external_sources.finding: Trace grading helps debug workflow-level issues; once good behavior is known, datasets and eval runs make improvements repeatable.
external_sources.loop_implication: Use trace-style run records during investigation, then promote recurring checks into deterministic validators and scorecards.

external_sources.label: OpenAI Codex - Best practices
external_sources.finding: Reliability improves when Codex knows what good looks like, creates tests when needed, runs checks, confirms results, and reviews work.
external_sources.loop_implication: AGENTS.md, adapter docs, and validation scripts must define done criteria for every build mode.

external_sources.label: OpenAI Codex - Custom instructions with AGENTS.md
external_sources.finding: Codex layers global and project instructions, with closer project files overriding earlier guidance.
external_sources.loop_implication: Install the loop into each build through AGENTS.md plus a local adapter so current, legacy, and greenfield projects inherit the same operating rules.

external_sources.label: Anthropic Claude Code - Hooks reference
external_sources.finding: Hooks can run at lifecycle points, block risky actions, inject context, and report asynchronous test results back into the agent workflow.
external_sources.loop_implication: Weekly and per-edit automations should run validations, surface failures, and block promotion when protected gates fail.

external_sources.label: Anthropic Claude Code - Create custom subagents
external_sources.finding: Specialized agents can research, review, debug, and test with scoped tools and instructions.
external_sources.loop_implication: Frame should model the loop as role-specific skills: scout, categorizer, investigator, auditor, fixer, verifier, implementer, and growth reporter.

external_sources.label: GitHub Docs - About Copilot cloud agent
external_sources.finding: Cloud agents can research repositories, plan, fix bugs, improve tests, update docs, and track PR lifecycle metrics such as created, merged, and median time to merge.
external_sources.loop_implication: Track cycle-time metrics for our own agent work, not just content metrics: request-to-build, build-to-review, review-to-ship, and fix-reopen rate.

external_sources.label: GitHub Docs - Responsible use of Copilot cloud agent
external_sources.finding: Responsible cloud-agent use emphasizes limited permissions, repository boundaries, signed/traceable commits, and ongoing security best practices.
external_sources.loop_implication: Automation needs clear permissions, traceability, branch/PR records, and human approval for public/client-facing changes.

external_sources.label: DORA - State of AI-assisted Software Development 2025
external_sources.finding: DORA frames AI as an amplifier of an organization system, not a magic replacement for the system.
external_sources.loop_implication: The business edge comes from better intake, specs, tests, reviews, release discipline, and learning loops around the tools.

external_sources.label: Google Cloud - Use Four Keys metrics to measure DevOps performance
external_sources.finding: Deployment frequency and lead time measure velocity, while change failure rate and time to restore service measure stability.
external_sources.loop_implication: Frame's factory dashboard should measure speed and stability together so faster delivery does not hide rework.

external_sources.label: NIST - AI Risk Management Framework
external_sources.finding: NIST positions AI risk management as voluntary, trustworthy, and tied to design, development, use, and evaluation.
external_sources.loop_implication: The audit stage should cover governance, known limits, human review, data boundaries, and evaluation records.

external_sources.label: OWASP - Top 10 for Large Language Model Applications
external_sources.finding: LLM applications face security risks such as prompt injection and insecure output handling.
external_sources.loop_implication: Any agent that can write files, send outreach, change public pages, or ingest untrusted web content needs prompt-injection and output-handling checks.

external_sources.label: xAI - X For You Feed Algorithm
external_sources.finding: The open For You feed implementation uses a composable recommendation pipeline with query hydration, candidate sources, hydrators, filters, scorers, selectors, post-selection checks, and side effects.
external_sources.loop_implication: Innovate research should rank opportunities like candidates: source them, hydrate with context, filter weak or risky items, score value/risk/reuse, select top actions, convert side effects into artifacts, and measure feedback.

external_sources.label: Twitter/X - Recommendation Algorithm
external_sources.finding: The earlier X algorithm release separates candidate sources, light/heavy rankers, home mixing, visibility filters, trust and safety models, user signals, graph features, and embeddings.
external_sources.loop_implication: The skill needs separate candidate sourcing, ranker, trust/safety, visibility, and user-signal feedback concepts instead of a single blended recommendation step.

external_sources.label: Microsoft/Recommenders - Best Practices on Recommendation Systems
external_sources.finding: The project frames recommendation work as prepare data, model, evaluate, optimize, and operationalize.
external_sources.loop_implication: Innovate outputs should include candidate preparation, comparison/evaluation, optimization, and operationalization instead of only a top-five action list.

external_sources.label: TensorFlow Recommenders
external_sources.finding: TensorFlow Recommenders provides a model-building workflow for recommender systems with data preparation, retrieval tasks, candidate metrics, training, and evaluation.
external_sources.loop_implication: The skill should separate retrieval-style candidate discovery from ranking and require evaluation over the candidate set.

external_sources.label: TensorFlow Ranking
external_sources.finding: TensorFlow Ranking includes pointwise, pairwise, and listwise losses; common ranking metrics such as MRR and NDCG; and unbiased learning-to-rank from biased feedback.
external_sources.loop_implication: The skill needs ranking metrics, bias-aware scoring, and explicit penalties for vendor hype, repeated-source bias, recency bias, and popularity bias.

external_sources.label: Metarank - Real-Time Personalization as a Service
external_sources.finding: Metarank integrates customer interaction signals like clicks and purchases into ranking and adapts search/recommendation results from feedback.
external_sources.loop_implication: The skill needs a feedback ledger so shipped actions are re-ranked based on real build outcomes, not initial research confidence alone.

external_sources.label: Feast - Open Source Feature Store
external_sources.finding: Feast manages offline and online features, supports low-latency serving, and emphasizes point-in-time correct historical feature sets to avoid leakage.
external_sources.loop_implication: The skill needs an evidence feature store: source/date/confidence at decision time, stale evidence markers, and no silent overwriting of prior evidence.

external_sources.label: NVIDIA Merlin Systems
external_sources.finding: Merlin Systems composes feature stores, nearest-neighbor search, filtering, ranking models, exploration strategies, and serving pipelines.
external_sources.loop_implication: The skill needs an exploration budget and serving-minded side effects: proven actions, monitored experiments, and reversible watchlist items.

external_sources.label: Meta Faiss
external_sources.finding: Faiss provides efficient similarity search, clustering, evaluation, and parameter tuning over large vector sets.
external_sources.loop_implication: The skill needs semantic recall before creating new artifacts: search prior docs, templates, skills, audits, and handoffs for reusable patterns.

external_sources.label: Vespa
external_sources.finding: Vespa is used for online search, vector search, recommendation, personalization, and RAG-style serving.
external_sources.loop_implication: The skill should distinguish offline research ranking from online build-serving surfaces like MCP resources, dashboards, and build adapters.

external_sources.label: RecBole
external_sources.finding: RecBole provides a unified framework with many algorithms, benchmark datasets, extensible data structures, and standard evaluation protocols.
external_sources.loop_implication: The skill needs repeatable eval cases and comparable score outputs so improvements can be benchmarked instead of judged subjectively.

external_sources.label: Gorse Recommender System Engine
external_sources.finding: Gorse imports items, users, and feedback; trains recommendations; exposes APIs; and provides a dashboard for monitoring and data management.
external_sources.loop_implication: The skill needs candidate/action, build/client, and feedback records that can later become API/dashboard data.

external_sources.label: DSPy
external_sources.finding: DSPy treats language-model workflows as programs that can be optimized from measured outputs rather than hand-prompted indefinitely.
external_sources.loop_implication: The skill needs eval-driven prompt/program improvement so successful outputs become reusable skill guidance and weak outputs become test cases.

external_sources.label: OpenAI Evals
external_sources.finding: OpenAI Evals is a framework and registry for evaluating LLMs and LLM systems, including custom evals for workflow-specific patterns.
external_sources.loop_implication: The skill needs an eval harness with broad strategy, implementation loop, compliance-sensitive, self-application, and current platform-shift cases.

research_snapshot.thesis: AI-builder content becomes a competitive advantage only when it is converted into a measured factory loop: research, categorize, investigate, audit, fix, re-audit, implement, and measure.

strengths: Research is converted into artifact targets instead of remaining passive reading.
Contractor-growth categories have a 9+/10 knowledge floor and validation.
The loop includes customer request automation as part of delivery speed.
The loop uses speed and stability metrics together.
open_risks: Outbound request delivery needs production email/SMS/Sheet provider credentials before real sends.
Factory dashboard metrics are visible, but booking-to-live and lead-quality deltas need more real customer cycles.
Per-client source-of-truth syncing still needs production access policies.
World-class local history is visible; scheduled remote Supabase sync still needs credentialed rollout.
loop_health.next_upgrade: Enable scheduled remote Supabase sync after service credentials, RLS verification, and owner approval are in place.

operating_principles: No research without a source and artifact target.
No broad audit without a candidate registry, score vector, decision, and filter reason for rejected ideas.
No evidence overwrite: preserve source/date/confidence at decision time and mark stale records explicitly.
No final top action list without bias and diversity checks.
No new durable artifact before semantic recall against prior docs, templates, skills, handoffs, validators, and MCP resources.
No category without business value, owner gates, risks, metrics, and tests.
No fix without the validation delta it is meant to close.
No implementation promotion without re-audit proof.
No public contractor claim, review, credential, service area, outreach, ad, GBP change, tracking script, or lead-data workflow without approval gates.
No speed metric without a paired stability metric.
No repeated manual blocker without turning it into a customer request template or automation.
No recurring research win without promotion into a template, skill, validator, MCP resource, AGENTS.md rule, or dashboard metric.

stages.label: Research Signal Intake
stages.objective: Collect high-signal YouTube, official docs, platform updates, competitive examples, and client-result signals without turning noise into build work.
stages.cadence: Weekly, plus before major architecture, SEO, AEO, automation, or offer changes.

metrics.label: Tracked sources refresh without failures
Research snapshot age
metrics.target: 7 days or less for weekly loop

metrics.label: Every candidate has source, date, and implication

quality_gates: Every external claim has a source URL or is marked as an internal inference.
Official docs or platform policy beat creator claims when they conflict.
No creator content is copied, downloaded, rehosted, or treated as ours.
failure_modes: Chasing viral tools that do not improve contractor builds.
Using stale tool behavior after AI platforms change.
Confusing content popularity with customer business value.
fix_policy: Demote unsourced claims to watchlist.
Refresh official docs for any compliance, API, MCP, Codex, Claude, Google, GBP, or search policy claim.
Attach an artifact target before promoting a pattern.
audit_policy: Weekly source failure check.
Monthly review of sources that stopped publishing or stopped being useful.
Quarterly source mix review across Codex, Claude, GitHub, Google, OpenAI, local SEO, and contractor growth.

stages.label: Categorize Into Business Value
stages.objective: Turn raw signals into product categories, contractor-growth modules, and build modes so agents know what each lesson should change.

metrics.label: P0 categories cover lead, authority, proof, tracking, QA, and compliance
Contractor category knowledge score floor
Categories mapped to concrete build artifacts

quality_gates: Every category includes modules, automations, owner gates, risks, metrics, MVP actions, and tests.
Every P0 category has an implementation destination.
Backlink, review, GBP, and outreach categories include compliance constraints.
failure_modes: A category describes a trend but not a product capability.
A useful lesson is trapped in docs and never reaches an issue, skill, or build gate.
A contractor category falls below the 9/10 knowledge threshold.
fix_policy: Raise weak categories with official sources and concrete tests.
Split overloaded categories into separate skills when the owner, data, and gates differ.
Reject categories that cannot name a business outcome.
audit_policy: Run category validation after generation.
Audit category gaps against shipped build failures and customer blockers.
Retire categories that stay watchlist-only for 90 days without value.

stages.label: Investigate Build Impact
stages.objective: Compare prioritized lessons against current builds, old builds, and greenfield starters to find the real operational gap.
stages.cadence: Weekly for platform; per client before strategy lock, launch, or monthly growth report.

metrics.label: Investigations ending with an artifact target
Customer blockers represented in request rows
Time from signal to impact decision

quality_gates: A lesson is not actionable until it names the affected artifact and mode.
Customer-facing asks must map to a request row, approval gate, or blocked reason.
Competitive lessons must be translated into our product language.
failure_modes: Research adds work that does not reduce delivery time or improve results.
Agents bounce between Claude/Codex without a single source of truth.
Customer missing-data loops remain manual and invisible.
fix_policy: Create a customer request row for missing facts, photos, headshots, proof, access, or approvals.
Create one Codex/Claude handoff with acceptance criteria instead of repeated vague reviews.
Defer non-P0 ideas to watchlist when they do not reduce a known bottleneck.
audit_policy: Every weekly P0 lesson gets an impact decision.
Every active client build gets a blocker audit before the next implementation sprint.
Every customer request row has owner, channel, status, and due date.

stages.label: Audit Against World-Class Gates
stages.objective: Score the build or capability against quality, speed, SEO/AEO, conversion, trust, compliance, customer friction, and maintainability gates.
stages.cadence: Before fixes, before launch, and after any major platform capability change.

metrics.label: World-class gate score
Findings include file/artifact and acceptance test
Compliance and approval risks are explicit

quality_gates: Findings lead with risks, not summaries.
Every P0 finding has an owner and a validation command or checklist.
Public-facing contractor claims, reviews, service areas, credentials, ads, GBP, and outreach are approval-gated.
failure_modes: Review loops become opinion loops with no measurable stop condition.
Speed hides quality regressions.
AI-generated changes create compliance, privacy, or trust problems.
fix_policy: Group findings by artifact so fixes can be owned cleanly.
Convert vague findings into acceptance tests before coding.
Block launch when compliance, tracking, or lead capture P0s are unresolved.
audit_policy: Re-run audit after fix stage.
Record residual risk in handoff docs.
Run contractor launch QA before public launch.

stages.label: Fix With Bounded Ownership
stages.objective: Implement the smallest high-value change that closes the audited gap while preserving user edits and local project conventions.
stages.cadence: After audit ranking and acceptance tests are defined.

metrics.label: Fix touches only necessary artifacts
Fix has mapped acceptance checks
P0 finding reopens after fix

quality_gates: Use existing patterns before adding new abstractions.
Preserve unrelated user changes.
Add tests or validators when the behavior is shared or recurring.
failure_modes: Fixes expand into broad rewrites.
Agents undo each other's changes.
Fixes satisfy code review but not customer delivery speed.
fix_policy: Assign disjoint write scopes when multiple agents are involved.
Keep customer-facing facts unchanged unless approved.
Update handoffs and adapters when the fix changes operating behavior.
audit_policy: Record the validation delta the fix is meant to close.
Run the narrowest reliable checks first, then broader checks before final handoff.
If checks fail, route back to investigate with the exact failure.

stages.label: Re-Audit And Prove Delta
stages.objective: Confirm the fix closed the original gap, did not create a new one, and improved the measurable score or cycle time.
stages.cadence: Immediately after fixes and before implementation promotion.

metrics.label: Original P0/P1 deltas closed
metrics.target: 100% or explicitly deferred with owner approval

metrics.label: New regressions from fix
Verification includes command, screenshot, or checklist evidence

quality_gates: Never mark a loop complete without stating the check that passed.
Browser/visual changes need rendered verification.
If customer input is missing, request it instead of inventing facts.
failure_modes: Review says fixed but tests were not run.
The same issue bounces between Claude and Codex because no delta is captured.
A passed local check hides a broken customer workflow.
fix_policy: Return failed checks to the fix stage with exact output.
Return ambiguous product questions to customer request rows.
Escalate repeatedly failing P0s into a handoff with fresh context.
audit_policy: Keep the original finding visible until proof exists.
Track reopened findings by category and agent.
Include residual risk in final handoff.

stages.label: Implement Into Builds And Skills
stages.objective: Promote proven lessons into reusable skills, build adapters, MCP resources/tools, customer requests, templates, QA gates, and starter workflows.
stages.cadence: After re-audit passes; weekly for adapter refresh; monthly for skill/template promotion.

metrics.label: Lesson promoted to reusable artifact when applicable
Current/legacy/greenfield guidance exists
Claude/Codex handoff includes context, files, tests, risks

quality_gates: A proven lesson is added to the adapter if it should affect future builds.
MCP feed exposes the loop to agents that do not read docs manually.
Customer outbound requests are generated for missing inputs instead of leaving silent blockers.
failure_modes: A fix works once but never becomes part of the factory.
Old builds and greenfield builds drift into different standards.
Operators forget the research because it is not in the build context.
fix_policy: Install or refresh the adapter in active builds after loop changes.
Update AGENTS.md when an operating rule changes.
Create a skill when the same workflow is repeated three times.
audit_policy: Weekly check confirms adapter files match global loop snapshot.
Monthly check asks what shipped quality improved because of the loop.
Quarterly prune unused skills/templates.

stages.label: Measure Business Outcomes And Feed Back
stages.objective: Close the loop by measuring delivery speed, quality, visibility, customer friction, lead outcomes, and reusable knowledge created.
stages.cadence: Weekly internal, monthly per client, quarterly system review.

metrics.label: Days from booking to launch-ready build
metrics.target: target 7-10 days for standard contractor build

metrics.label: Days blocked waiting for customer input
metrics.target: less than 3 active days per build stage

metrics.label: Build changes requiring rollback or major rework
Leads tied back to source/city/service/page

quality_gates: Measure speed and stability together.
Separate vanity metrics from qualified leads and booked jobs.
Feed recurring misses back into research and customer request automation.
failure_modes: The factory ships faster but customer input still blocks launches.
Reports show traffic but not booked work.
The same missing asset or approval appears across clients.
fix_policy: Create reusable customer request templates for repeated missing inputs.
Turn recurring delivery delays into factory automation tasks.
Turn recurring ranking/lead gaps into contractor growth category updates.
audit_policy: Weekly factory review: speed, failures, blockers, shipped lessons.
Monthly client review: visibility, leads, proof, authority, next actions.
Quarterly competitor review: categories where we are below 9/10.

competitive_categories.label: AI Build Process And Factory Speed
competitive_categories.current_edge: The loop makes speed measurable from booking to launch while preserving audit/re-audit gates.
competitive_categories.next_edge: Add factory dashboard metrics for request-to-build, build-to-review, review-to-live, and blocked-by-customer time.

competitive_categories.label: Codex, Claude, MCP, Skills, And Agent Ops
competitive_categories.current_edge: Agent roles, AGENTS.md instructions, hooks/validators, and MCP feeds keep context consistent across tools.
competitive_categories.next_edge: Add role-specific skills for investigator, auditor, verifier, and customer-request dispatcher.

competitive_categories.label: Local Contractor Growth System
competitive_categories.current_edge: The contractor category map already covers lead capture, tracking, GBP, SEO/AEO, proof, reviews, backlinks, and monthly growth.
competitive_categories.next_edge: Connect category gaps directly to city/service/product launch pages and monthly authority tasks.

competitive_categories.label: AI Visibility, SEO, AEO, And Authority
competitive_categories.current_edge: The loop ties AI-search readiness and backlink authority to ethical source-backed growth categories.
competitive_categories.next_edge: Add citation/source monitoring for AI Overviews, ChatGPT referrals, Bing/IndexNow, and local citations.

competitive_categories.label: Customer Request And Delivery Layer
competitive_categories.current_edge: The loop treats missing customer facts, photos, access, approvals, and proof as data rows, not ad hoc messages.
competitive_categories.next_edge: Generate email/SMS/Sheet digests and sync replies back into the same request rows.

competitive_categories.label: Risk, Compliance, And AI Security
competitive_categories.current_edge: NIST/OWASP/OpenAI/GitHub/Google policy sources inform approval gates, prompt-injection checks, and human review.
competitive_categories.next_edge: Add automated risk tags to agent actions that touch outreach, public pages, reviews, GBP, tracking, credentials, or lead data.

competitive_categories.label: Knowledge Packaging, Training, And Reuse
competitive_categories.current_edge: Research becomes reusable docs, adapters, MCP resources, and skill candidates instead of isolated chat output.
competitive_categories.next_edge: Create a quarterly skill promotion review and operator training path from the loop outputs.

competitive_categories.label: Auto-Enhance Design And Conversion Polish
competitive_categories.current_edge: Design improvements are treated as a measurable lane with screenshots, category audit, bounded patches, browser QA, and reusable patterns.
competitive_categories.next_edge: Expose auto-enhance design actions in every build adapter and promote winning visual/component improvements into starters.

competitive_categories.label: Recommendation Pipeline Prioritization
competitive_categories.current_edge: Research candidates are filtered and scored before they become build work, reducing tool-chasing and opinion-based prioritization.
competitive_categories.next_edge: Add explicit candidate scoring to broad innovate audits and platform build-intelligence panels.

competitive_categories.label: Evidence Memory And Feedback Ledger
competitive_categories.current_edge: Research decisions preserve source/date/confidence at decision time and can be re-ranked after build outcomes are known.
competitive_categories.next_edge: Add candidate registry, evidence feature store, and shipped-action feedback ledger to the platform.

competitive_categories.label: Bias-Aware Evaluation And Diversity Gates
competitive_categories.current_edge: Broad audits penalize vendor hype, popularity bias, recency bias, repeated-source bias, and category collapse before selecting actions.
competitive_categories.next_edge: Add bias/diversity checks and eval cases to every high-stakes innovate run.

competitive_categories.label: Semantic Recall And Prompt Optimization
competitive_categories.current_edge: Before creating new artifacts, the loop searches prior docs/templates/skills/handoffs and turns successful outputs into reusable guidance.
competitive_categories.next_edge: Add local semantic recall and eval-driven skill optimization to reduce duplicate work.

action_backlog.title: Expose the build intelligence loop in the MCP feed and resource list.
acceptance_tests: Feed includes operating_loop, loop_health, and loop_actions.
Resource list includes docs/build-intelligence-loop.
action_backlog.business_value: Keeps every active build chat anchored to the operating loop.

action_backlog.title: Add loop generation and validation to the weekly trigger.
acceptance_tests: Weekly log shows loop generated and validated.
Validator fails if stages or sources are incomplete.
action_backlog.business_value: Prevents the factory from decaying into stale docs.

action_backlog.title: Create a customer request dispatcher skill that turns blockers into email/SMS/Sheet-ready requests.
action_backlog.destination: skills/customer-request-dispatcher or platform request delivery module
acceptance_tests: Requests include owner, channel, due date, artifact, approval gate, and sync-back key.
action_backlog.business_value: Cuts back-and-forth by making missing input visible and routable.

action_backlog.title: Add delivery speed and stability metrics to the platform dashboard.
acceptance_tests: Metrics include booking-to-live, customer-wait time, review-reopen rate, and change failure rate.
action_backlog.business_value: Lets Frame manage the factory like an operating system, not a collection of chats.

action_backlog.title: Expose world-class research history, feedback ledger status, and remote-sync gates in the platform dashboard.
action_backlog.destination: customer-build-platform world-class intelligence panel and Supabase build_intelligence_* tables
acceptance_tests: Dashboard shows local latest/history snapshots with readiness, candidate, evidence, eval, and feedback counts.
Remote sync is visible as approval-gated and draft-only until signed Supabase credentials are configured.
Database eval-case constraint supports broad_strategy, implementation_loop, compliance_sensitive, self_application, and current_search_shift.
action_backlog.business_value: Turns the research loop into visible operational memory instead of a hidden JSON artifact.

action_backlog.title: Install the loop adapter into old builds and generate a scored upgrade queue before editing pages.
acceptance_tests: Legacy mode queue ranks proof, CTA, schema, tracking, service-area, and approval gaps.
action_backlog.business_value: Turns old sites into structured upgrades instead of rewrites.

action_backlog.title: Bake the loop into the greenfield contractor starter before sitemap/copy/design generation.
acceptance_tests: Starter includes loop README, queue, AGENTS.md block, QA gates, and request templates.
action_backlog.business_value: Makes new builds faster from the first prompt.

action_backlog.title: Promote repeated loop roles into skills: scout, categorizer, investigator, auditor, fixer, verifier, integrator, reporter.
acceptance_tests: Each skill has trigger, inputs, outputs, safety rules, and validation commands.
action_backlog.business_value: Reduces agent drift and makes the system trainable.

action_backlog.title: Add prompt-injection and insecure-output checks for agents touching public pages, outreach, reviews, GBP, or lead data.
acceptance_tests: Risk tags are present for untrusted input and public-output actions.
action_backlog.business_value: Protects the system while automation increases.

action_backlog.title: Run the Auto-Enhance Design Lane before launch QA when visual quality, trust proof, or mobile conversion is weak.
acceptance_tests: Desktop and mobile render without overlap.
CTA remains obvious and forms/calls still work.
Reusable visual pattern is added to a template, brief, or backlog when it should affect future builds.
action_backlog.business_value: Improves first-impression trust and conversion without reopening the whole build.

action_backlog.title: Use Recommendation Pipeline Mode for broad audits so ideas are sourced, hydrated, filtered, scored, selected, converted into side effects, and measured.
action_backlog.destination: innovate-research skill and build-intelligence action selection
acceptance_tests: Broad audits include source/hydrate/filter/score/select/side-effect/feedback logic.
Selected candidates include artifact target, approval gate, acceptance check, and measurement path.
High-risk or missing-fact candidates become Monitor or Request input instead of Ship.
action_backlog.business_value: Improves prioritization quality and reduces low-value research churn.

action_backlog.title: Add a candidate registry and evidence feature store for broad innovate runs.
action_backlog.destination: innovate-research skill, build-intelligence feed, Supabase build_intelligence_candidates and build_intelligence_evidence tables
acceptance_tests: Every candidate records source URL, source date, observed date, confidence, category, artifact target, approval gate, decision, acceptance check, and measurement path.
Evidence is append-only at decision time and stale evidence is marked instead of overwritten.
Rejected candidates keep filter reasons so the system learns what not to repeat.
action_backlog.business_value: Creates durable research memory and prevents repeated manual rediscovery.

action_backlog.title: Add bias and diversity gates before selecting top research actions.
action_backlog.destination: innovate-research skill and build review scorecards
acceptance_tests: Broad audits check vendor bias, popularity bias, recency bias, repeated-source bias, category collapse, survivorship bias, and feedback bias.
Top-five action lists include at least three distinct categories unless the user requests a narrow lane.
High-bias candidates become Monitor or Skip unless corroborated.
action_backlog.business_value: Improves decision quality and keeps the system from chasing noisy sources.

action_backlog.title: Run semantic/local recall before creating new docs, skills, templates, validators, or handoffs.
action_backlog.destination: docs, templates, .agents/skills, MCP resources, handoff files
acceptance_tests: Before creating an artifact, search prior docs/templates/skills/handoffs for reusable patterns.
If a similar artifact exists, update it instead of duplicating it.
Reuse decision is recorded in the final handoff.
action_backlog.business_value: Reduces duplicate work and compounds prior research.

action_backlog.title: Create eval cases for broad strategy, implementation loop, compliance-sensitive, self-application, and current platform-shift innovate runs.
action_backlog.destination: innovate-research skill eval notes and future test fixtures
acceptance_tests: Eval cases cover broad_strategy, implementation_loop, compliance_sensitive, self_application, and current_search_shift scenarios.
Eval cases verify candidate scoring, artifact targets, acceptance checks, HYPE/Skip classification, and approval gates.
Compliance-sensitive cases reject fake reviews, spam, fake engagement, and unapproved claims.
Self-application cases require every skill category to score at least 9/10 and run deterministic validators.
Failed outputs become skill improvement tasks.
action_backlog.business_value: Makes the skill measurable and improvable instead of prompt-only.

action_backlog.title: Add a feedback ledger and exploration budget for shipped research actions.
action_backlog.destination: build-intelligence feedback table, platform dashboard, monthly growth digest
acceptance_tests: Shipped actions record validation result, cycle-time delta, quality delta, visibility/lead delta, rework delta, and customer-wait delta.
Broad audits allocate roughly 70% proven actions, 20% monitored experiments, and 10% exploratory watchlist.
Recurring wins are promoted into templates, skills, validators, MCP resources, or AGENTS.md rules.
action_backlog.business_value: Turns research into a self-improving operating system.

action_backlog.title: Run executable evals against saved innovate-research outputs instead of only checking skill file keywords.
acceptance_tests: Five eval cases pass: broad_strategy, implementation_loop, compliance_sensitive, self_application, and current_search_shift.
Each case validates saved output structure, sources where needed, approval gates, HYPE/Skip coverage, artifacts, and measurement.
action_backlog.business_value: Makes skill quality observable from outputs, not just instructions.

action_backlog.title: Turn feedback ledger rows into adjusted future action scores.
acceptance_tests: Feedback scores include base score, adjustment, adjusted score, measurement status, and recommended decision.
Future priority queues sort by adjusted score instead of first-pass score alone.
action_backlog.business_value: Closes the loop between measured results and what the factory works on next.

action_backlog.title: Check generated research reports for dates, source URLs, NEW-section evidence, and optional live link health.
acceptance_tests: Reports have Generated dates inside the accepted freshness window.
Reports include source URLs and NEW sections include evidence.
Optional network mode can verify live URL health before major decisions.
action_backlog.business_value: Reduces stale research and unsupported current-platform claims.

action_backlog.title: Keep remote Supabase world-class history blocked until credentials, RLS verification, and explicit owner approval exist.
acceptance_tests: Remote sync returns approval_required before touching Supabase unless BUILD_INTELLIGENCE_SYNC_REMOTE_APPROVED=1 or --approve-remote is present.
UI/feed exposes local_only, approval_required, or remote_requested state.
Remote writes remain draft-only and public_side_effect_allowed=false.
action_backlog.business_value: Lets remote memory turn on cleanly without weakening Supabase safety.

action_backlog.title: Package the loop as a plugin only after it proves itself across multiple real build modes.
acceptance_tests: Readiness gate requires at least three world-class history snapshots.
Readiness gate requires at least two build modes represented.
Plugin package excludes credentials and public-side-effect automations.
action_backlog.business_value: Prevents premature packaging while giving the loop a clean plugin path.

current.objective: Expose the loop through MCP, active build context, customer request rows, QA gates, and dashboard metrics.

legacy.objective: Install the adapter, audit before edits, and generate a ranked upgrade queue.

greenfield.objective: Install the loop before sitemap/copy/design so every new build starts with current research, gates, and request templates.

score_components.description: Loop reduces missing-input and approval delay.
Vendor, popularity, recency, repeated-source, and category-collapse bias are checked.
Lessons become adapters, skills, templates, or MCP tools.
Shipped actions are re-ranked from real outcomes.
Measures leads, rankings, booked jobs, cycle time, and rework.
